title: LRRK2 Staining in mouse brain sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6nj8m","text":"LRRK2 is a widely expressed, multifunctional protein.LRRK2 mutations are the major cause to inherited and sporadic PD. Staining of LRRK2 in mouse brain can be challenging. This protocol provides an optimised way to achieve LRRK2 staining in mouse brain sections. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":53,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Blocking with 5% serum (preferably same serum as host of secondary antibody) 30 min RT
VECTOR® antigen unmasking solution 48ul/5ml water (in small glass sample vial). Microwave 10-20sec until boiled before use (or pre-heat to 100°C on heat plate)
Incubate at 100°C for 30min (<5 section / 5ml) then cool to room temperature (about 20min)
Primary incubation 1% serum + 0.1% Triton 30-48 hr 4°C LRRK2 1:200 (Catalog number: N241A); Co-stained often with TH antibody
Secondary incubation 1% serum + 0.1% Triton 1-2 hr RT Alexa Fluor 1:300
Mount with ProLong™ Gold Antifade Mountant with DAPI
creator: divya.darwinarulseeli
